content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Applied all 4 pending amendments: (1) reverted engine.rs runtime log format from error={:?} (Debug) back to error={} (Display) to eliminate double-escaping of stderr/stdout tails embedded in error messages; (2) removed the attempt_number.saturating_add(1) hack from the stale-session retry path and instead added a push_arg() method to PreparedCommand, using it to inject --verbose directly into the rebuilt command args — this preserves attempt_number's semantic meaning as the engine's external retry counter; (3) added bounded stdout_tail (STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT) to the Codex 'missing last-message, stdout fallback not valid JSON' error path so the actual malformed stdout body is visible in the runtime log, plus a regression test finish_codex_stdout_fallback_invalid_includes_stdout_tail; (4) verified via claude --help that --verbose ('Override verbose mode setting from config') is the correct flag for moderate extra diagnostics in -p --output-format json mode, while --debug would produce a firehose of internal API/hooks output that overwhelms the bounded stderr snippets.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Revert engine.rs runtime log format from error={:?} (Debug) to error={} (Display) at line 5068 to eliminate double-escaping of embedded stderr/stdout tails in error messages
2. [Completed] Add push_arg(&mut self, arg: String) method to PreparedCommand (process_backend.rs line 62) to allow injecting args after build_command
3. [Completed] Remove attempt_number.saturating_add(1) from stale-session retry path; instead inject --verbose directly via fresh_prepared.push_arg() gated on BackendFamily::Claude (process_backend.rs line 1472-1479)
4. [Completed] Add bounded stdout_tail (STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT) to the Codex 'missing last-message file, stdout fallback not valid JSON' error path (process_backend.rs line 346-347) alongside existing stderr_tail
5. [Completed] Add regression test finish_codex_stdout_fallback_invalid_includes_stdout_tail verifying stdout_tail and stderr_tail presence and content in error message (process_backend.rs line 3763)
6. [Completed] Verify Claude CLI --verbose flag via claude --help: confirmed --verbose is the correct moderate-diagnostics flag; --debug produces excessive internal output unsuitable for bounded stderr capture
7. [Completed] Run cargo test (757 pass, 0 fail), cargo clippy (0 new warnings in modified files), cargo fmt --check (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- 7 diagnostic-specific tests pass: finish_claude_invalid_envelope_includes_stdout_len_and_stderr, finish_claude_invalid_result_json_includes_stdout_len_and_stderr, build_command_claude_omits_verbose_on_first_attempt, build_command_claude_includes_verbose_on_retry, finish_codex_invalid_last_message_includes_output_sizes, finish_codex_stdout_fallback_invalid_includes_stdout_tail, build_command_claude_schema_has_no_refs_or_definitions
- 757 total tests pass across all test targets (0 failures)
- cargo fmt --check passes with no formatting issues
- cargo clippy shows 0 new warnings in modified files (engine.rs, process_backend.rs)
- Claude CLI --help confirms --verbose is 'Override verbose mode setting from config' (moderate output) vs --debug which is 'Enable debug mode with optional category filtering' (firehose)

## Outstanding Risks

- Codex CLI has no equivalent --verbose flag; transient Codex failures still rely on captured stderr and runtime/failed/*.failed.raw artifacts for full diagnosis beyond the bounded snippets now included in error messages
- Claude CLI --verbose semantics are documented only as 'Override verbose mode setting from config'; if a future CLI version changes what verbose mode emits to stderr in -p --output-format json mode, the diagnostic value may change — but the bounded truncation ensures no impact on runtime log size
